message:
perf: cache project docs at init, replace snapshot with lightweight meta

Two performance improvements to the session loop:

1. Project docs (LoadProjectDocs) were called every round, spawning a
   `git rev-parse` subprocess each time. Now loaded once in initSessionState()
   and cached in session fields for the lifetime of the session.

2. maybeAutoSave() wrote the full session history as indented JSON every
   round, growing linearly. Now writes a ~500-byte SessionMeta (compact
   JSON, no history). History is already durably recorded by the append-only
   transcript JSONL. RestoreSessionFromMeta recovers history exclusively
   from the transcript.

SessionSnapshot and SaveSession/LoadSession are retained (deprecated) for
backward compatibility with external tools (transcript_tools.go, serfeval).
The recall strategy explicitly saves a full snapshot when it needs to search
session history.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
